field.role: tells you what exists (field / substrate / scalar manifold state)
erdos_applications: Density of primes and prime gaps
Distribution of integers in additive sets

shear.role: tells you how it deforms (shear / metric deformation / lawful geometry)

packet.role: tells you what is emitted/witnessed (packet / executable typed glyph-witness / codec event)

spectral.role: tells you what basis survives (spectral / eigenbasis / pruning-correlation structure)

erdos_turan_conjecture.name: Erdős–Turán Conjecture on Additive Bases
erdos_turan_conjecture.statement: If A is an additive basis of order 2 for the natural numbers, then the sum of reciprocals diverges: Σ_{a∈A} 1/a = ∞
erdos_turan_conjecture.mapping: Additive basis density = field distribution. Conjecture about density of basis elements.
erdos_turan_conjecture.approach: Treat A as density field ρ(n). Analyze spectral decomposition of additive structure. Use field primitive to model basis density and shear primitive to analyze additive deformation.
erdos_turan_conjecture.feasibility: HIGH - Directly about density/distribution, maps cleanly to field primitive

erdos_straus_conjecture.name: Erdős–Straus Conjecture
erdos_straus_conjecture.statement: For every integer n ≥ 2, the equation 4/n = 1/x + 1/y + 1/z has a solution in positive integers x, y, z
erdos_straus_conjecture.mapping: Egyptian fraction decomposition = packet encoding. Each solution is a packet (x,y,z) encoding 4/n.
erdos_straus_conjecture.approach: Treat solutions as packets. Use packet primitive to search for encoding space. Spectral analysis of solution space structure.
erdos_straus_conjecture.feasibility: HIGH - Problem about finding encodings/packets, natural fit for packet primitive

erdos_conjecture_arithmetic_progressions.name: Erdős Conjecture on Arithmetic Progressions
erdos_conjecture_arithmetic_progressions.statement: If Σ_{a∈A} 1/a diverges, then A contains arbitrarily long arithmetic progressions
erdos_conjecture_arithmetic_progressions.mapping: Divergent reciprocal sum = high density field. High density implies rich structure (APs).
erdos_conjecture_arithmetic_progressions.approach: Use field primitive to model density ρ(A). Apply shear primitive to analyze how density deforms under translation (arithmetic progression structure). Spectral decomposition to detect periodic structure.
erdos_conjecture_arithmetic_progressions.feasibility: HIGH - Directly about density implying structure, field primitive natural fit

erdos_renyi_random_graph.name: Erdős–Rényi Random Graph Model
erdos_renyi_random_graph.statement: Study properties of G(n,p) random graphs. Threshold phenomena for connectivity, giant component, Hamiltonicity
erdos_renyi_random_graph.mapping: Random graph eigenvalue distribution = spectral basis. Phase transitions = spectral pruning.
erdos_renyi_random_graph.approach: Use spectral primitive to analyze eigenvalue distribution of G(n,p). Detect phase transitions via spectral gap. Field primitive for density of edges.
erdos_renyi_random_graph.feasibility: VERY HIGH - Well-studied, spectral methods standard, direct mapping

erdos_ko_rado.name: Erdős–Ko–Rado Theorem (extensions)
erdos_ko_rado.statement: Maximum size of intersecting families of k-subsets of {1,...,n}
erdos_ko_rado.mapping: Intersecting family = packet collection with witness property (intersection).
erdos_ko_rado.approach: Treat each family as packet set. Use packet primitive to analyze encoding constraints. Spectral analysis of intersection graph.
erdos_ko_rado.feasibility: MEDIUM - Solved for large n, but extensions open. Packet primitive useful for generalizations

erdos_szekeres.name: Erdős–Szekeres Theorem (generalizations)
erdos_szekeres.statement: Any sequence of n²+1 distinct real numbers contains a monotone subsequence of length n+1
erdos_szekeres.mapping: Monotone subsequence = packet witness. Ramsey-type problem about finding structure.
erdos_szekeres.approach: Use packet primitive to model subsequences as witnesses. Shear primitive for ordering deformation. Spectral analysis of permutation patterns.
erdos_szekeres.feasibility: MEDIUM - Solved, but generalizations and extensions open

erdos_ginzburg_ziv.name: Erdős–Ginzburg–Ziv Theorem (extensions)
erdos_ginzburg_ziv.statement: Any 2n-1 integers contain n whose sum is divisible by n
erdos_ginzburg_ziv.mapping: Zero-sum subset = packet with witness property (sum = 0 mod n).
erdos_ginzburg_ziv.approach: Treat subsets as packets. Use packet primitive to search for zero-sum encoding. Spectral analysis of additive structure modulo n.
erdos_ginzburg_ziv.feasibility: MEDIUM - Solved, but extensions to other groups and structures open

erdos_stone.name: Erdős–Stone Theorem (extremal function)
erdos_stone.statement: For any graph H, ex(n,H) = (1 - 1/χ(H)-1 + o(1))n²/2 where χ(H) is chromatic number
erdos_stone.mapping: Extremal function = shear metric. Maximum edges without H = deformation constraint.
erdos_stone.approach: Use shear primitive to analyze edge density under forbidden subgraph constraint. Spectral analysis of extremal graphs. Field primitive for density.
erdos_stone.feasibility: MEDIUM - Solved, but generalizations to hypergraphs open

erdos_faber_lovasz.name: Erdős–Faber–Lovász Conjecture
erdos_faber_lovasz.statement: If each edge of a complete graph on n vertices is colored with one of n colors, then there exists a set of n edges with no two sharing a vertex or having the same color
erdos_faber_lovasz.mapping: Edge coloring = packet encoding. Matching = packet set with witness properties.
erdos_faber_lovasz.approach: Use packet primitive to model edge colorings as encodings. Spectral analysis of intersection graph. Shear for matching constraints.
erdos_faber_lovasz.feasibility: EXPLORATORY - Recently solved (2021), but method could generalize

erdos_distinct_distances.name: Erdős Distinct Distances Problem
erdos_distinct_distances.statement: Any set of n points in the plane determines at least n/√log n distinct distances
erdos_distinct_distances.mapping: Distance set = shear metric. Point configuration = field manifold.
erdos_distinct_distances.approach: Use field primitive for point configuration. Shear primitive for distance metric. Spectral analysis of distance distribution.
erdos_distinct_distances.feasibility: EXPLORATORY - Solved (Guth-Katz), but 4-primitive approach could provide new perspective

erdos_moser_problem.name: Erdős–Moser Problem
erdos_moser_problem.statement: Find all solutions to 1/a + 1/b + 1/c + 1/d + 1/e = 1 in distinct positive integers
erdos_moser_problem.mapping: Egyptian fraction decomposition = packet encoding. Each solution is a 5-tuple packet.
erdos_moser_problem.approach: Use packet primitive to search for encoding space. Spectral analysis of solution structure. Field for density of solutions.
erdos_moser_problem.feasibility: EXPLORATORY - Solved (only known solution), but method could generalize to other Diophantine equations

erdos_hadamard.name: Erdős Hadamard Conjecture
erdos_hadamard.statement: There exist Hadamard matrices of order 4k for all k
erdos_hadamard.mapping: Hadamard matrix = spectral basis (orthogonal rows/columns). Eigenvalues = ±√n.
erdos_hadamard.approach: Use spectral primitive to analyze matrix structure. Field for existence density. Packet for construction methods.
erdos_hadamard.feasibility: EXPLORATORY - Open problem, spectral methods standard in Hadamard matrix theory

insights.packet_dominance: Packet primitive dominates (5 problems) - many Erdős problems are about encodings/witnesses
insights.spectral_validation: Erdős–Rényi provides validation point - spectral methods standard
insights.field_additive: Field primitive for additive problems - density and structure
insights.shear_extremal: Shear primitive for extremal problems - metric and deformation
insights.cross_domain: All primitives represented - framework covers diverse Erdős problem types
